Title: Regulation of nucleus-encoded trans-acting factors allows orthogonal fine-tuning of multiple transgenes in the chloroplast of Chlamydomonas reinhardtii

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: nac2-26-derived engineered strains (e.g., METE_N#5, MN_dc#1, CSB195#1 derivatives)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: METE promoter (vitamin B12-repressible; synthetic regulation in nucleus)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Vitamin B12 supplementation (picomolar to nanomolar/µM range); thiamine as orthogonality control
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Synthetic nuclear expression in complemented lines; function read out via chloroplast psbD-dependent photosynthetic growth and reporter transgenes carrying psbD 5' UTR
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered chloroplast/cell culture with dodecane overlay extraction
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful orthogonal trans-acting factor for fine-tuning chloroplast transgenes carrying psbD 5' UTR; can be combined with MRL1 system to independently tune multiple pathway genes during metabolic engineering
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPR protein; nucleus-encoded chloroplast trans-acting factor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Vitamin B12-repressible NAC2 reduces stabilization/expression of psbD 5'UTR-dependent chloroplast transgenes, thereby lowering reporter output and casbene synthase-driven casbene production
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant complementation; vitamin dose-response growth assays; RT-qPCR of psbD; reporter assays (NanoLuc, mVenus); metabolic profiling of casbene
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAC2 placed under the B12-repressible METE promoter enabled selective repression of psbD-dependent chloroplast gene expression. 2. The NAC2 system acted orthogonally to the thiamine-responsive MRL1 system. 3. Repression of NAC2-controlled JcCS expression decreased casbene production, demonstrating utility for pathway tuning.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PMETE-mediated repression was incomplete, leaving basal expression even at high B12; this limits full shutoff of target transgenes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary role in this paper is as a synthetic regulator rather than direct metabolic enzyme; effect on casbene is indirect through control of JcCS expression

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: mrl1-5-derived engineered strains (e.g., T4M#5, MN_dc#1, CSB195#1 derivatives)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: THI4_4N riboswitch-controlled nuclear expression; in some constructs target chloroplast transgenes use rbcL or wendy1 5' UTRs
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thiamine supplementation (nanomolar to micromolar range); vitamin B12 as orthogonality control
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Synthetic nuclear expression in complemented lines; function read out via chloroplast rbcL- or wendy1-UTR-dependent reporters and pathway transgenes
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered chloroplast/cell culture with dodecane overlay extraction
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Orthogonal regulator for tuning chloroplast transgenes carrying rbcL or wendy1 5' UTRs; useful for independently controlling precursor-supply or product-forming genes in pathway engineering
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PPR protein; nucleus-encoded chloroplast trans-acting factor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Thiamine-repressible MRL1 reduces stabilization/expression of rbcL- or wendy1-dependent chloroplast transgenes, enabling near-complete repression of reporter output and strong reduction of casbene or precursor-supply effects
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant complementation; vitamin dose-response growth assays; RT-qPCR of rbcL; reporter assays (NanoLuc, mVenus); CFP-tagged DXS monitoring; casbene GC-MS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MRL1 under a thiamine-responsive riboswitch enabled selective repression of chloroplast genes using rbcL or wendy1 5' UTRs. 2. This system was orthogonal to the B12-responsive NAC2 system. 3. Repressing MRL1-controlled SpDXS reduced the DXS-dependent increase in casbene, confirming its utility for pathway-flux control.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First demonstration in this paper of using MRL1 as a tunable chloroplast transgene regulator; effect on metabolism is indirect via regulation of JcCS or SpDXS expression

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; increased expression alone did not further increase casbene in all contexts, suggesting precursor limitation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: CSB195#1-derived transformants (e.g., CSB340, CSB341, CSB342, CSB353, CSB354)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: psbD promoter/5'UTR; wendy1 promoter/5'UTR; rbcL promoter/5'UTR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Vitamin B12 or thiamine supplementation for transgene repression; TAP medium cultivation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Synthetic chloroplast expression as mVenus fusion; detectable under psbD and wendy1 control but not rbcL in this context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Culture dodecane overlay/extracellular organic phase after production in cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression of JcCS enabled de novo casbene production in chloroplast-engineered Chlamydomonas; repression of JcCS expression lowered casbene titre
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chloroplast transgenic expression; mVenus fluorescence; vitamin-regulated expression assays; GC-MS quantification of casbene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JcCS expression in the chloroplast produced detectable casbene when driven by psbD or wendy1 regulatory regions, but not by the tested rbcL configuration. 2. Higher transgene dosage at the double-copy psbA locus increased reporter fluorescence but did not increase casbene titre. 3. Vitamin-mediated repression of JcCS expression proportionally reduced casbene production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key product-forming enzyme for chloroplast diterpenoid engineering; should be paired with precursor-supply engineering because raising expression alone may not maximize titre
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher expression/copy number did not improve casbene titre, indicating substrate limitation and possible diminishing returns
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: JcCS was expressed as a codon-optimized mVenus fusion; lack of expression from the rbcL 5'UTR construct may relate to translation start context incompatibility

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream precursor-supply step for plastidial isoprenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate and glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose 5-phosphate (DXP)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a pathway-flux control point for boosting casbene precursor supply in this system
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: wendy1 promoter/5'UTR or psbD promoter/5'UTR depending on construct
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thiamine or vitamin B12 supplementation depending on regulatory configuration; TAP medium cultivation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Synthetic chloroplast expression as CFP fusion; detectable when under wendy1/MRL1 control in CSB353, not detectably expressed in reciprocal CSB354 context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Culture dodecane overlay/extracellular organic phase after production in cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Additional SpDXS expression increased casbene titre approximately twofold by enhancing precursor flux; repression of SpDXS reduced casbene to baseline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-transgene chloroplast expression; CFP fluorescence as proxy for protein accumulation; vitamin repression assays; GC-MS quantification of casbene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpDXS overexpression under the functional wendy1/MRL1 module increased casbene production about twofold. 2. Thiamine repression eliminated CFP signal and reduced casbene to baseline, confirming causality. 3. The reciprocal construct failed to show detectable SpDXS expression, indicating construct context strongly affects chloroplast transgene function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply engineering target to boost diterpenoid output; should be co-optimized with product synthase expression and regulatory context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported at maximal expression; expression was highly context-dependent, with one construct giving no detectable protein
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DXS function was tested in addition to endogenous DXS, not as replacement; evidence supports precursor limitation in the engineered casbene pathway
